feedback: Progress was made on integrating Manus-related logic and setting up automation, but the actual code changes are not visible, key dependencies (e.g., MANUS_API_KEY) remain unset, and no test coverage or verification of new functionality is provided. The implementation appears incomplete and may contain regressions.
issues: No visible diff or concrete changes in core/dispatch/run.py; only edits recorded.
MANUS_API_KEY may not be set, risking runtime failures.
Scripts and cron jobs created but not verified to work correctly.
No unit or integration tests added for new logic.
Potential edge cases (e.g., missing session ID, malformed API responses) not handled.
Unclear how the new Manus logic integrates with existing worker configurations.

feedback: The session shows progress toward automating the Manus daily credit refresh, including script creation, secret handling, and cron setup. However, the actual implementation details of the script, error handling, and verification steps are missing or incomplete. No test files were added, and there is no evidence that the changes were validated or that edge cases (e.g., existing cron entries, secret retrieval failures) are handled.
issues: No diff provided, making it unclear what exact changes were made to run.py or other files.
The manus-daily-refresh.sh script was created and edited but its contents and idempotency are not demonstrated.
Cron entry creation logic only checks for existence but does not guarantee proper formatting or permissions.
Secrets handling uses raw string literals without escaping; potential injection or truncation issues.
No unit or integration tests were added for the new script or modified modules.
Error paths (e.g., missing secrets, failed playwright install) are not captured or logged.
No verification that the cron job actually runs or that credits are correctly refreshed.
Potential race condition when appending to crontab if multiple runs occur simultaneously.

feedback: The session shows productive work on integrating Manus daily credit refresh, including script creation, cron setup, and code compilation. However, there is no evidence of test coverage for the new or modified code, and the listed test gaps remain unaddressed. Edge cases are not explicitly handled, and the changes appear to be functional but not fully vetted.
